title: teucrium commune var. pseudoaureum
intro: Teucrium commune var. pseudoaureum, commonly known as Golden Germander, is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region and is a variant of the more widely known Teucrium chamaedrys. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects. The health benefits of Golden Germander include its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to alleviate digestive problems and reduce stress. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or essential oil, making it a versatile herbal remedy for a range of health concerns.
